results.possibleIdentity: John Trotter was a technical professional and engineer active in the late 1960s and 1970s, based in Downey, California. He was a computer programmer and aeronautical engineer, fields highly relevant to Downey’s status as a major aerospace hub during that era. In the late 1970s, he was associated with "Artisan Software," a venture based at 6429 Dos Rios Road in Downey, and was an active member of the Pascal User's Group, as documented in their 1978 newsletters. His professional background aligns with the goals of the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) organization, which fostered collaborations between artists and engineers. Additionally, records from the mid-1970s indicate his presence in Downey, where he utilized his technical expertise to address local community issues, such as noise pollution measurement. Given his specialized fields and location, he likely worked for major aerospace contractors in the region, such as North American Rockwell, or was affiliated with the University of Southern California (USC).
